abstract: Despite the foundational role of faith, many contemporary Christian families struggle with unhappiness due to a lack of understanding regarding biblical family management. This study explores Psalm 127 as a central framework for establishing a joyful household aligned with divine will, positing that family life devoid of spiritual involvement inevitably lacks true fulfillment. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive methodology, the research examines the theological implications of God’s sovereignty over the domestic sphere. The findings suggest that endeavors independent of God result in futility, whereas acknowledging His ultimate authority invites divine intervention. Ultimately, a family that center’s its life on God’s leadership experiences genuine happiness, as He actively works within the home to cultivate lasting joy and peace.
